mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates directly lower the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) against rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 7.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and imminent recession, triggering risk-off portfolio rebalancing that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 7.0, or the signal loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline by at least 0.05 IC, or forward 60-day returns are negative in over 60% of co-expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index above 0.5 percent reflects accelerating factory output, which generates short-term momentum trading flows that push S&P 500 prices higher within one week.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return is negative or flat in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the signal's predictive accuracy vanishes outside recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL reading above 230.0 indicates entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in over 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.0, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL levels and returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, mechanically boosting corporate revenues and driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a lower out-of-sample R-squared than either proxy alone, or forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 55% of months satisfying both conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated housing starts signal peak construction activity and impending economic deceleration, prompting algorithmic trading systems to front-run the downturn and generate negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of high-HOUST observations, or the signal's rank correlation with returns falls below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: High industrial production coinciding with low federal funds rates creates an optimal growth environment where output expansion is not constrained by borrowing costs, producing strong positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal underperforms a market-neutral benchmark by more than 0.5% monthly, or forward returns are negative in over 60% of triggered months despite favorable macro conditions.
